zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Mortimer於1304年5月2日出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾維格莫 卡素時，她的父親Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March17歲，母親Joan de Geneville18歲。她於1319年5月與Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1337年5月5日在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊享年33歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭布里斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Mortimer于1304年5月2日出生在英国英格兰賀福爾維格莫 卡素时，她的父亲Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March17岁，母亲Joan de Geneville18岁。她于1319年5月与Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1337年5月5日在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊享年33岁，埋葬在英国英格兰布里斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Mortimer was born on 2 May 1304, in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, was 17 and her mother, Joan de Geneville, was 18. She married Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston in May 1319, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 5 May 1337, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 33, and was buried in Bristol, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Mortimer est née le 2 mai 1304, à Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Joan de Geneville, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston en mai 1319, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 mai 1337, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 33 ans, et a été enterrée à Bristol, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Mai 1304 in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March 17 und ihre Mutter Joan de Geneville 18. Sie hat im Mai 1319 in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. Mai 1337 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Bristol, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston nel maggio 1319, nel Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 5 maggio 1337, nel Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 33 anni, e fu sepolta a Bristol, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Mortimerが1304年5月2日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモー カストルで生まれた時，父Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of Marchは17歳，母Joan de Genevilleは18歳であった。1319年5月にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでSir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Costonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1337年5月5日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーで33歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブリストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Mortimer(이)가 1304년 5월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 위그모어 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Geneville(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1319년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1337년 5월 5일 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 33세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 브리스톨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mortimer nasceu em 2 de maio de 1304, em Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Geneville, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston em maio de 1319, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 5 de maio de 1337, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 33 anos, e foi sepultada em Bristol, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Mortimer родилась 2 мая 1304 г. в местности вигмор кастл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Geneville, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Berkeley Lord of Coston май 1319 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 5 мая 1337 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 33 лет и была похоронена в местности бристол, Англия, Великобритания.
